ucs.title: [LLM] ALPHV/BlackCat Rust encryptor launch — `--access-token` flag + safe-boot pre-encryption pivot
ucs.description: Hunts the very specific CLI argument grammar of the Rust-built ALPHV/BlackCat (Noberus) encryptor — the binary refuses to decrypt its embedded config without an `--access-token` value, and operators routinely chain `--safeboot[-network]`, `--propagated`, `--no-vm-kill` and `--child` flags. Catching this string set on a process create is the highest-fidelity host-side signal that the ransomware binary itself has just been staged on a victim, often seconds before VSS deletion + reboot-to-safe-mode (T1656/T1564.001) which BlackCat uses to bypass EDR.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process="*--access-token*" (Processes.process="*--paths*" OR Processes.process="*--propagated*" OR Processes.process="*--child*" OR Processes.process="*--safeboot*" OR Processes.process="*--no-vm-kill*" OR Processes.process="*--bypass*" OR Processes.process="*--ui*") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.user _time span=1s | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval token_len=len(cmdline) | where token_len > 80
ucs.kql:
// ALPHV/BlackCat Rust binary launch — argument grammar is unique
let alphv_args = dynamic(["--access-token","--paths ","--propagated","--child","--safeboot","--safeboot-network","--no-vm-kill","--bypass","--no-prop","--log-file","--no-thread-priority"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"                               // skip machine accounts
| where ProcessCommandLine has "--access-token"                  // mandatory ALPHV flag
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (alphv_args)
| extend FlagCount = array_length(extract_all(@"(?i)--[a-z\-]+", dynamic(["--"]), ProcessCommandLine))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName,
          FolderPath,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentUser  = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          IsRemote    = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// ALPHV/BlackCat Rust binary launch (Sentinel SecurityEvent EID 4688)
let alphv_args = dynamic(["--access-token","--paths ","--propagated","--child","--safeboot","--safeboot-network","--no-vm-kill","--bypass","--no-prop","--log-file","--no-thread-priority"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has "--access-token"
| where CommandLine has_any (alphv_args)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: ALPHV/BlackCat (Noberus) Rust Encryptor Launch via --access-token
id: 6f3a2b9d-7e1c-4b2a-9f8d-2b3c1f0a4e72
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of the ALPHV/BlackCat (Noberus) ransomware encryptor; the Rust binary requires an --access-token CLI argument that decrypts the embedded config, paired with operator flags such as --safeboot, --propagated and --child.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/06/13/the-many-lives-of-blackcat-ransomware/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/unc2596-cuba-ransomware
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-061a
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_token:
    CommandLine|contains: '--access-token'
  selection_args:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--paths '
      - '--propagated'
      - '--child'
      - '--safeboot'
      - '--safeboot-network'
      - '--no-vm-kill'
      - '--bypass'
      - '--no-prop'
      - '--no-thread-priority'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_token and selection_args and not filter_machine
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Internal red-team using leaked ALPHV builder
  - Threat-intel sandbox detonating BlackCat samples
level: critical
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1564.001
  - attack.t1656
ucs.rationale: The `--access-token` argument is hard-coded into the BlackCat/ALPHV Rust binary as a runtime decryption key for its embedded config — without it the binary refuses to encrypt. This is documented by Microsoft (DEV-0237/0504), Mandiant, Sophos and CISA AA23-061A. No legitimate Windows tooling uses the same flag combo (`--access-token` + `--paths`/`--propagated`/`--safeboot`). The `--safeboot[-networ

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat affiliate BYOVD — POORTRY/STONESTOP signed kernel driver service install for EDR kill
ucs.description: Hunts BlackCat (UNC4466 / FIN7-overlap) affiliate tradecraft of dropping a Microsoft-signed-but-malicious kernel driver (POORTRY a.k.a. ktgn.sys/kmsdrv.sys, sometimes paired with the STONESTOP user-mode loader) and registering it as a service to terminate EDR/AV processes from kernel space immediately before staging the ALPHV encryptor. This combines T1543.002 (Windows service creation) with T1014 (rootkit) and T1574.006 — the technique chain Mandiant attributed publicly to ALPHV affiliates in late-2022 / early-2023 intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.process_name) as proc values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("ktgn.sys","kmsdrv.sys","poortry.sys","stonestop.exe","tjr.exe","ProcExp.sys","ProcExp152.sys") OR (Filesystem.file_path="*\\System32\\drivers\\*" Filesystem.file_name="*.sys" Filesystem.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name _time span=1s | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Registry.registry_key_name) as svc_key values(Registry.registry_value_data) as ImagePath from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_key_name="*\\Services\\*" Registry.registry_value_name="ImagePath" Registry.registry_value_data="*\\drivers\\*" by Registry.dest _time span=120s | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | rename _time as svc_time] | where abs(svc_time - lastTime) < 120
ucs.kql:
// BlackCat affiliate POORTRY/STONESTOP driver drop + service registration (within 2 min)
let known_byovd_drivers = dynamic(["ktgn.sys","kmsdrv.sys","poortry.sys","procexp.sys","procexp152.sys","rentdrv2.sys","truesight.sys","viragt64.sys"]);
let known_byovd_loaders = dynamic(["stonestop.exe","tjr.exe","poortry.exe"]);
let DriverDrops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where (FileName in~ (known_byovd_drivers))
         or (FolderPath has @"\System32\drivers\" and FileName endswith ".sys"
             and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","msiexec.exe"))
         or (FileName in~ (known_byovd_loaders))
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DriverPath = FolderPath, DriverName = FileName,
              DriverSHA256 = SHA256, DropperUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              DropperImage = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let ServiceCreates =
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (@".sys", @"\drivers\")
    | project SvcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              ServiceKey = RegistryKey, DriverImagePath = RegistryValueData,
              SvcUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              SvcParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
DriverDrops
| join kind=inner ServiceCreates on DeviceId
| where SvcTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 2m)
| project DropTime, SvcTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SvcTime, DropTime),
          DeviceName, DropperUser,
          DropperImage, DropperCmd,
          DriverName, DriverPath, DriverSHA256,
          ServiceKey, DriverImagePath, SvcParent
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackCat BYOVD driver drop + service install (Sentinel ASIM)
let known_byovd_drivers = dynamic(["ktgn.sys","kmsdrv.sys","poortry.sys","procexp.sys","procexp152.sys","rentdrv2.sys","truesight.sys","viragt64.sys"]);
let DriverDrops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where (TargetFileName in~ (known_byovd_drivers))
         or (TargetFilePath has @"\System32\drivers\" and TargetFileName endswith ".sys"
             and ActingProcessName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","msiexec.exe"))
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              DriverPath = TargetFilePath, DriverName = TargetFileName,
              DriverSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256, DropperUser = ActorUsername,
              DropperImage = ActingProcessName, DropperCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine;
let ServiceCreates =
    ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Services\"
    | where RegistryValue =~ "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (@".sys", @"\drivers\")
    | project SvcTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              ServiceKey = RegistryKey, DriverImagePath = RegistryValueData,
              SvcUser = ActorUsername, SvcParent = ActingProcessName;
DriverDrops
| join kind=inner ServiceCreates on DvcHostname
| where SvcTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 2m)
| project DropTime, SvcTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SvcTime, DropTime),
          DvcHostname, DropperUser, DropperImage, DropperCmd,
          DriverName, DriverPath, DriverSHA256, DriverImagePath, SvcParent
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: Mandiant (UNC4466) and SentinelOne publicly attributed the POORTRY/STONESTOP signed-driver toolkit — abusing leaked Microsoft attestation-signing certs — to BlackCat/ALPHV affiliates as the EDR-kill step immediately preceding deployment of the Rust encryptor. Driver names ktgn.sys / kmsdrv.sys, paired with a service `ImagePath` registry write within ~2 minutes of the drop and a non-installer paren
